purpose: Section-specific revise prompt for revising a draft gates field that failed its rubric. Composed at runtime with system_prompts/revise.json.
system_addendum:
SECTION YOU ARE REVISING: gates

The gates field is an array of { point, check, blocking } triples. The previous draft failed at least one dimension of the V1 gates rubric. Your revision must address every failing dimension while preserving every passing one.

DIMENSION-BY-DIMENSION REVISION GUIDANCE

If lifecycle_point_valid failed (≤ 1):
  - Check each gate's point against the enum: pre_commit, pre_merge, pre_deploy, release, quarterly_review, annual_review, incident_response. Project-tier principles gate at pre_merge / pre_deploy. Enterprise-tier at release / quarterly_review / annual_review. Re-check ownership.tier and align.

If check_is_binary_and_verifiable failed (≤ 1):
  - Check text uses recommendation language ('should', 'ensure', 'maintain'). Rewrite as verifiable conditions: 'eval/refresh/config.yaml MUST exist and declare X, Y, Z', 'PR diff under src/agent/ MUST include matching change to eval/refresh/log.yaml'. A reader / tool should be able to determine pass/fail mechanically.

If required_status_check_constraint_present failed (≤ 1):
  - For project-tier CI gates, ADD the verbatim branch-protection language: 'Configured as a required status check on the integration branch (typically `develop` in git-flow setups, `main` in trunk-based setups) via branch protection — advisory CI runs do not satisfy this gate. Failure blocks merge.' This is the catalogue's hard rule from taxonomy.json gates_spec.required_status_check_constraint.

If blocking_correctness failed (≤ 1):
  - CI-enforced project-tier gates are blocking: true (almost always). Non-blocking is reserved for advisory monitors or warnings. If a gate is non-blocking, it's either advisory (which is rare and should be documented) or the blocking value is wrong.

If coverage_completeness failed (≤ 1):
  - Re-read solution.approach. List every artefact / path / condition it names. For each, identify which gate enforces it. Any artefact without a gate needs one added (or an explicit note in solution.approach that the artefact is generated/audit-only, not gated).

If parallel_form_with_siblings failed (≤ 1):
  - Compare against sibling gates sections. Match gate count (1-3 typically), structure { point, check, blocking }, branch-protection boilerplate phrasing, vocabulary. Do not invent new gate shapes if a sibling has established the convention.

WHAT TO PRESERVE

The rubric scores tell you which dimensions passed. Preserve those. If only required_status_check_constraint_present failed, you only need to add the branch-protection language — don't change the gate's point, check substance, or blocking value.
user_template:
The previous gates draft failed its rubric. Revise it.

<principle_id>{{ principle_id }}</principle_id>
<ownership_tier>{{ ownership_tier }}</ownership_tier>

<aws_anchor>
  <best_practice>{{ aws_bp }}</best_practice>
  <implementation_step>
    <number>{{ aws_step_number }}</number>
    <title>{{ aws_step_title }}</title>
  </implementation_step>
</aws_anchor>

<statement>
  <title>{{ statement_title }}</title>
  <description>{{ statement_description }}</description>
</statement>

<solution>
  <approach>{{ solution_approach }}</approach>
</solution>

<siblings_under_same_bp>
{{ sibling_gates }}
</siblings_under_same_bp>

<failing_draft>
  <gates>{{ candidate_gates }}</gates>
</failing_draft>

<rubric_scores>
  <lifecycle_point_valid score="{{ score_lifecycle_point_valid }}">{{ justification_lifecycle_point_valid }}</lifecycle_point_valid>
  <check_is_binary_and_verifiable score="{{ score_check_is_binary_and_verifiable }}">{{ justification_check_is_binary_and_verifiable }}</check_is_binary_and_verifiable>
  <required_status_check_constraint_present score="{{ score_required_status_check_constraint_present }}">{{ justification_required_status_check_constraint_present }}</required_status_check_constraint_present>
  <blocking_correctness score="{{ score_blocking_correctness }}">{{ justification_blocking_correctness }}</blocking_correctness>
  <coverage_completeness score="{{ score_coverage_completeness }}">{{ justification_coverage_completeness }}</coverage_completeness>
  <parallel_form_with_siblings score="{{ score_parallel_form_with_siblings }}">{{ justification_parallel_form_with_siblings }}</parallel_form_with_siblings>
</rubric_scores>

Return the JSON object specified in the output contract — same shape as the generate output, drop-in replacement.

schema.gates: array of { point: string, check: string, blocking: boolean }
